note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's <break> tags and sentence pauses (never rewritten, summarized, or reordered). Block windows are MEASURED onsets, not the nominal formula: Sigma(ffprobe per-file audio + 0.4) puts the s10 end at 167.500s, which overshoots the ffprobe-probed master (167.158s) by 0.343s (> 0.3 tolerance) because the concatenated MP3 frames were joined with ~0.36s inter-block gaps rather than 0.4s. Onsets were therefore measured by normalized FFT cross-correlation of each WC-PB-09-sNN.mp3 (8 kHz mono PCM) against the joined master; all 10 blocks matched at score 1.000. Master ffprobe duration 167.158s; audioDurationS in the lesson JSON is set to that probed value.

phrases.text: Open Reports from the left rail.
Every report a site runs lives in this one hub.
A hundred seventy-three in all, five favorites, three on a schedule.
Each row carries a type tag and the last time it ran.
Name the symptom before you touch a filter.
Wrong numbers or an empty report is a filter question.
A scheduled report that never landed is a scheduler question.
Decide which ticket you have, or you'll re-run the wrong report ten times.
Take the wrong-or-empty trail first. Open the report from the list.
It loads in the Report Viewer, and the filter bar across the top is the whole story.
More filters, a location selector, a date range, and the run control.
Now read the scope.
Most wrong-numbers tickets die here.
The selector sat on one site when the manager meant the network,
or the dates missed the week in question.
Fix both, run it again, compare.
A report is only wrong once its filters are right.
One caution before you file anything.
In this demo tenant, a KPI tile and the table beneath it often disagree, sometimes wildly.
That's a known demo artifact, not a platform bug.
Reproduce the numbers in production before you open a ticket.
Now the report that never arrived. Open the Scheduled Reports view.
Three reports run on a schedule here.
Confirm the missing one is actually among them, and read its last run.
Not scheduled at all? There's your answer.
Cross-check the job that sends it. Settings, then Automation, then Task Scheduler.
Eighteen tasks, sixteen active, two running now.
And the tile that matters reads failed, zero.
Nothing errored overnight.
When that count isn't zero, you have your lead.
Find the Daily Report Email row.
Read across it.
Scheduled daily at six. Last ran today at six.
Next run, tomorrow at six. Status, active.
A missing report usually shows here as a stale last run or a status that isn't active.
Read it, never edit it.
Now build the ticket.
Name the report, the location scope, and the date range you ran it with.
For a scheduled report, add the job's last run, next run, and status.
Attach a screenshot. Leave the demo-tenant number quirks out of it.
That's the playbook.
Name the symptom.
Wrong or empty means the filter bar.
Never arrived means Scheduled Reports and the Task Scheduler.
Rule out the demo artifact, escalate with the filters and the run times,
and change nothing along the way.
